summary: The user is planning a solo hike in the Bay Area, specifically on the Dipsea Trail, which they have chosen for its challenging terrain and stunning views. They discussed various trail options, water sources, and trail conditions, ultimately deciding on a route that includes starting at the Steep Ravine Trailhead, hiking to the summit of Mount Tamalpais, and returning via the Steep Ravine Trail.
facts: The user is planning a solo hike this weekend in the Bay Area. The user has recently hiked the Steep Ravine Trail on Mount Tamalpais. The user is looking for a longer hike of 7-10 miles with similar difficulty and views to the Steep Ravine Trail. The user has chosen to hike the Dipsea Trail, which is 7.4 miles out-and-back with an elevation gain of 1,300 feet. The user plans to start at the Steep Ravine Trailhead and take the Dipsea Trail to the summit of Mount Tamalpais. The user will descend via the Steep Ravine Trail. The user is aware that there are no potable water sources along the Dipsea Trail and plans to bring 2-3 liters of water. The user knows there are restrooms at the Steep Ravine Trailhead but none along the Dipsea Trail. The user is prepared for steep dropoffs, loose gravel, and other obstacles on the Dipsea Trail. The user is looking forward to the views from the summit of Mount Tamalpais.
